title: ericoides umbellatum
intro: Ericoides umbellatum, also known as Uva-Ursi or Bearberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Northern Europe, Asia, and North America, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and red berries. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and inflammation, due to its high content of arbutin and other compounds. The health benefits of Uva-Ursi include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off bacterial and fungal infections, and support the immune system. Uva-Ursi can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues.
